title: hypericum perforatum subsp. vulgare
intro: Hypericum perforatum subsp. vulgare, commonly known as wild St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species in the genus Hypericum. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of wild St. John's Wort include its potential to treat mild to moderate depression, anxiety, and sleep disorders, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is often used in herbal medicine to support mental health and well-being.
